environment_notes.note: Build artifact present on disk. Confirmed gitignored via .gitignore:36 (__pycache__/). Not tracked, not part of the inspected diff. introduced_in_diff: false (classification corrected per codex feedback — causation by diff cannot be asserted for an ignored file). No cleanup required.

file_assessments.location: correct — hooks/ is canonical location for PreToolUse hooks
file_assessments.diff_summary: Adds 1 line: check_and_consume_allowlist invocation before systemctl block error output, enabling allowlist bypass for blocked systemctl commands. Legitimate security enhancement.

file_assessments.location: correct — hooks/ is canonical location for PreToolUse hooks
file_assessments.diff_summary: Refactors _evaluate_push signature from (command, sid) to (command, data) with internal SID extraction via _get_session_id(data). Adds _check_git_allowlist call before default-deny block. Updates call site in _evaluate_command to pass data instead of sid. Legitimate security/allowlist enhancement.

summary.verdict: Both changed files are well-placed, correctly named, actively maintained hooks with strong functional references. Zero cleanliness issues introduced in this diff.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed zero blocking cleanliness issues for the two hook files. Raised two findings: (1) introduced_in_diff: true for the .pyc file is misclassified since it is gitignored and not part of the tracked diff — should be introduced_in_diff: false; (2) the .pyc file is out-of-scope for --changed-files mode and should be in environment_notes, not findings. Two OBSERVATION_ONLY items: no Safety Rules violations from deletion (no deletion proposed), and the zero-blocking conclusion is correct.

findings.issue: introduced_in_diff: true for hooks/__pycache__/pretool-git-privilege-guard.cpython-312.pyc is misclassified — the file is gitignored and not part of the tracked diff, so causation by the diff cannot be asserted
findings.proposed_fix: Set introduced_in_diff: false/unknown. Mark as OBSERVATION_ONLY. No required cleanup.
findings.rationale: Corrected introduced_in_diff to false and moved .pyc entry to environment_notes rather than findings

findings.issue: In strict --changed-files mode scoped to the two hook files, the __pycache__ file is out-of-scope as a cleanliness finding
findings.proposed_fix: Move it from findings to environment_notes, or omit it entirely from the changed-files cleanliness result
findings.rationale: Moved the .pyc entry to environment_notes section where it serves as context without being a blocking finding

findings.issue: No proposed deletion in the draft, so no Safety Rules violation from deletion
findings.rationale: Confirmed no deletions are proposed; Safety Rules compliance verified

findings.issue: The draft's zero blocking cleanliness issues conclusion for the two hook files is correct
findings.rationale: Conclusion confirmed accurate by codex verification of hook paths, naming, tracked status, and diff content

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied both PROPOSED_FIX items: (1) corrected introduced_in_diff to false for the .pyc file, (2) relocated .pyc entry from findings to environment_notes
